sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.83 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: A generating system$X$of a group$G$is if there is an integer$n$such that every element of$G$can be expressed as a product of at most$n$elements of$X$or their inverses. If not, we say that it is slow. For instance, in$( ,+)$, the squares are fast, but the powers of 2 are slow. Do there exist countable infinite groups without an infinite slow generating set? Uncountable ones do exist.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
